sections.summary: This section is a document heading providing the title and adoption/amendment dates of the Codex Standard for Dairy Permeate Powders (CXS 331-2017), without substantive technical requirements.

This note states that, following a 2022 Codex decision, amendments were made to Section 7.2 on labelling of non-retail containers in the dairy permeate powders standard, without detailing the amended requirements.

This note records that, following a 2023 Codex decision, food additive provisions in the dairy permeate powders standard were amended and incorporated into the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), without specifying the substantive limits or conditions.
This scope section states that the standard applies to dairy permeate powders, as described in Section 2, intended for further processing and/or use as ingredients in other foods.

Defines dairy permeate powders as dried milk products with high lactose content obtained by removing milk fat and protein, but not lactose, from specified dairy raw materials by membrane filtration or equivalent processes, and distinguishes whey permeate powder and milk permeate powder by their respective permeate sources.

Introduces essential composition and quality factors for dairy, whey, and milk permeate powders, including their permitted raw materials, allowed ingredient (seed lactose), and required compositional criteria and limits, with allowance for certain compositional modifications within specified minima and maxima.

Specifies that dairy permeate powders must be made from milk, whey, cream, sweet buttermilk permeates or similar lactose-containing milk products, while whey permeate powder must be from whey permeate and milk permeate powder from milk permeate.
Allows the use of seed lactose as a permitted ingredient solely for manufacturing pre-crystallized dairy permeate powder products.

Sets minimum lactose and maximum nitrogen, milk fat, ash, and moisture contents for dairy, whey, and milk permeate powders, clarifies expression of lactose and moisture, and allows limited compositional modification (e.g. partial demineralization) provided specified minima and maxima are still respected.

Prohibits the use of food additives in dairy permeate powders covered by this standard and references Codex standards for definitions of related dairy terms (milk product, whey, cream, milk permeate).
Requires that any processing aids used in products covered by this standard comply with the Codex Guidelines on Substances Used as Processing Aids (CXG 75-2010).

Requires permeate powder products and the milk used to make their raw materials to comply with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins and with Codex maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides applicable to milk.

Recommends that products covered by this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with Codex hygienic texts (e.g., CXC 1-1969, CXC 57-2004 and other relevant codes) and comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

States that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), specific labelling provisions apply (see Section 7.1 name of the food and Section 7.2 non-retail container labelling).

Requires the name of the food to be 'dairy permeate powder', allows products meeting the Section 2 descriptions and Section 3.3 compositions to be named 'milk permeate powder' or 'whey permeate powder', and permits adding the designation 'lactose-rich deproteinized [dairy/whey/milk] powder' where appropriate in the country of sale.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) for checking compliance.
